Title: Assign ESI Level 1 for a patient requiring immediate life-saving intervention
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; an unassigned patient is checked into the ED queue
Test_Steps: 1. Open the triage assessment for the patient | 2. Indicate the patient requires immediate life-saving intervention (e.g. intubation) | 3. Confirm the acuity decision | 4. Save the triage record
Expected_Result: Patient is assigned ESI Level 1, flagged as Resuscitation, and moved to the top of the queue with an immediate-attention indicator

Title: Assign ESI Level 2 for high-risk presentation without immediate intervention
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; patient in ED queue
Test_Steps: 1. Open triage assessment | 2. Record a high-risk situation (severe pain or confusion) without need for immediate life-saving care | 3. Confirm acuity | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Patient is assigned ESI Level 2 (Emergent) and prioritized above levels 3-5 in the queue

Title: ESI Level 3 decision based on two-or-more resources prediction
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; vitals captured within danger-zone-safe range
Test_Steps: 1. Open triage assessment | 2. Record stable vitals | 3. Predict two or more resources required (labs + imaging) | 4. Confirm acuity | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Patient is assigned ESI Level 3 (Urgent) per the resource-prediction branch of the decision table

Title: ESI decision-table coverage for resource count 0, 1 and many
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; patient with stable vitals
Test_Steps: 1. Run triage with zero predicted resources -> expect Level 5 | 2. Run triage with one predicted resource -> expect Level 4 | 3. Run triage with two-plus resources and stable vitals -> expect Level 3
Expected_Result: System maps 0 resources to ESI 5, 1 resource to ESI 4 and 2+ resources to ESI 3 exactly per the ESI algorithm

Title: Danger-zone vitals upgrade an ESI Level 3 to Level 2
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; patient predicted to need 2+ resources
Test_Steps: 1. Begin triage with 2+ resources (tentative Level 3) | 2. Enter vitals that breach age-specific danger-zone thresholds | 3. Confirm acuity | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System upgrades the acuity from ESI 3 to ESI 2 due to danger-zone vital signs

Title: Reject triage save when acuity level is not selected
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; triage form open
Test_Steps: 1. Enter chief complaint and vitals | 2. Leave the acuity level unselected | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation message requiring an acuity level; no triage record is persisted

Title: Reject out-of-range acuity value submitted via API
Preconditions: API client authenticated with triage write scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST a triage observation with an acuity value outside 1-5 | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: API rejects the request with a 400/422 validation error and no observation is created

Title: Boundary acuity values 1 and 5 are accepted
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Save a triage with acuity = 1 | 2. Save a separate triage with acuity = 5 | 3. Verify both persisted
Expected_Result: Both boundary acuity values 1 and 5 are accepted and stored; values 0 and 6 are rejected

Title: Pediatric danger-zone thresholds differ from adult thresholds
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; patient age recorded as infant
Test_Steps: 1. Open triage for a 6-month-old | 2. Enter HR=160, RR=55 | 3. Confirm acuity
Expected_Result: System applies age-specific (pediatric) danger-zone bands and upgrades acuity accordingly rather than using adult thresholds

Title: Re-triage updates acuity and preserves prior assessment in history
Preconditions: Patient already triaged at ESI 3 and waiting
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient's triage history | 2. Initiate a re-triage | 3. Lower acuity to ESI 2 due to deterioration | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Current acuity becomes ESI 2, queue priority recalculates, and the prior ESI 3 assessment remains in the audit/history trail

Title: Route low-acuity ESI 4/5 patient to fast-track
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; fast-track area configured
Test_Steps: 1. Complete triage with ESI 4 | 2. Confirm disposition routing | 3. Verify queue assignment
Expected_Result: Patient is routed to the fast-track queue and removed from the main ED waiting list

Title: Block fast-track routing for high-acuity patient
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Complete triage with ESI 2 | 2. Attempt to manually route to fast-track
Expected_Result: System prevents routing an ESI 1/2 patient to fast-track and shows a safety warning

Title: Pairwise of acuity x complaint-category x age-band for fast-track eligibility
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; fast-track rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute the pairwise matrix combining acuity {4,5}, complaint {minor-injury, minor-illness}, age-band {adult, geriatric} | 2. Record routing decision per combination
Expected_Result: Each combination routes consistently with configured fast-track eligibility rules; geriatric high-risk combos are excluded as designed

Title: Fast-track capacity full diverts patient back to main queue
Preconditions: Fast-track area at maximum configured capacity
Test_Steps: 1. Triage an eligible ESI 4 patient | 2. Attempt fast-track routing | 3. Observe diversion
Expected_Result: Patient is held in the main queue with a capacity notice and is auto-routed when a fast-track slot frees up

Title: Capture full triage vitals set and persist as observations
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; patient checked in
Test_Steps: 1. Open triage vitals form | 2. Enter HR, RR, BP, Temp, SpO2, pain score | 3. Save
Expected_Result: All vitals are saved as discrete observations with correct units, timestamp and triage encounter linkage

Title: Reject physiologically impossible vital values
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; vitals form open
Test_Steps: 1. Enter HR=600 | 2. Enter SpO2=150 | 3. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Each impossible value is rejected with a validation message; no out-of-range observation is stored

Title: Boundary validation on temperature and SpO2 limits
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Temp at lower bound and upper bound | 2. Enter SpO2 at 0 and 100 | 3. Save valid bounds, reject beyond
Expected_Result: Values exactly at configured bounds are accepted; values one step beyond the bounds are rejected

Title: Auto-calculated MAP/derived value from BP entry
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; derived value configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter systolic and diastolic BP | 2. Save the vitals | 3. Inspect derived mean arterial pressure
Expected_Result: Derived MAP is computed and stored as a separate observation alongside the BP reading

Title: Abnormal vitals trigger visual danger-zone flag on triage form
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Enter SpO2=88 and RR=30 | 2. Observe form feedback before save
Expected_Result: The form visually flags the danger-zone vitals and surfaces a prompt to reconsider acuity

Title: Vitals captured at triage are exposed via FHIR Observation
Preconditions: Triage encounter with vitals saved; FHIR API enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET the patient's Observation resources filtered by vital-signs category | 2. Validate codes and values
Expected_Result: FHIR returns vital-signs Observations with correct LOINC codes, UCUM units, effectiveDateTime and encounter reference

Title: Higher-acuity patient sorts above lower-acuity in the ED queue
Preconditions: Two patients in queue at different acuities
Test_Steps: 1. Triage patient A at ESI 4 | 2. Triage patient B at ESI 2 | 3. View the ED queue ordering
Expected_Result: Patient B (ESI 2) is sorted above patient A (ESI 4) regardless of arrival order

Title: Tie-break by arrival time within the same acuity level
Preconditions: Two patients triaged at identical acuity
Test_Steps: 1. Triage patient A (earlier arrival) at ESI 3 | 2. Triage patient B (later arrival) at ESI 3 | 3. View queue order
Expected_Result: Within the same acuity, the earlier-arrival patient sorts first (FIFO tie-break)

Title: Manual queue override is permitted and audited
Preconditions: Charge nurse with override privilege; queue populated
Test_Steps: 1. Manually move a lower-acuity patient up the queue | 2. Provide override reason | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Override is applied, the reason is captured, and an audit entry records the actor, time and justification

Title: Queue override blocked for user without privilege
Preconditions: User without queue-override role logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to manually reorder the ED queue
Expected_Result: Override action is denied with an authorization error and no queue change occurs

Title: Long-wait low-acuity patient surfaces a wait-time escalation flag
Preconditions: ESI 4 patient waiting beyond configured threshold
Test_Steps: 1. Triage patient at ESI 4 | 2. Advance simulated clock past wait-time threshold | 3. Observe queue
Expected_Result: Patient is flagged for wait-time escalation/reassessment without altering the underlying acuity

Title: Concurrent triage of two patients does not corrupt queue order
Preconditions: Two triage nurses on separate sessions
Test_Steps: 1. Both nurses save triage for distinct patients simultaneously | 2. Inspect the persisted queue ordering and acuity records
Expected_Result: Both triage records persist atomically with correct ordering and no lost-update or duplicate-position anomaly

Title: Auto-escalate to physician when ESI 1 is assigned
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; escalation rules active
Test_Steps: 1. Assign ESI 1 to a patient | 2. Confirm | 3. Observe escalation
Expected_Result: An immediate physician notification/escalation is triggered and the patient state transitions to Resuscitation-Pending

Title: Sepsis screening positive triggers escalation bundle
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; sepsis screen configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter vitals/criteria meeting SIRS/sepsis screen | 2. Save triage
Expected_Result: Positive sepsis screen escalates the patient and prompts the sepsis pathway/bundle

Title: Stroke screen positive starts time-critical stroke pathway
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; stroke screen configured
Test_Steps: 1. Record positive FAST findings and last-known-well time | 2. Save
Expected_Result: Stroke alert is raised, last-known-well captured, and the time-critical stroke pathway is initiated

Title: No escalation fired for negative screening result
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in; screens configured
Test_Steps: 1. Complete triage with screening criteria all negative | 2. Save
Expected_Result: No escalation or pathway is triggered; patient follows standard acuity-based queueing

Title: Failed escalation notification is retried and logged
Preconditions: Notification channel temporarily unavailable
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an ESI 1 escalation | 2. Force the notification channel to fail | 3. Observe retry behavior
Expected_Result: Notification failure is logged, retried per policy, and a fallback alert path is engaged so the escalation is not silently lost

Title: Register an unidentified patient with a temporary trauma alias
Preconditions: Registration/triage clerk logged in; unknown-patient workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Start unknown-patient registration | 2. Generate a temporary alias and unknown-patient identifier | 3. Capture estimated age and sex | 4. Save and check in
Expected_Result: An unknown patient record is created with a unique temporary identifier and trauma alias, ready for immediate triage

Title: Merge unknown-patient record once true identity is confirmed
Preconditions: Unknown patient record exists with encounters; matching identified record found
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm true identity | 2. Initiate merge of the unknown record into the identified patient | 3. Verify encounters and observations carry over
Expected_Result: All triage encounters/observations are preserved and re-linked to the confirmed identity; temporary identifier is retired and the merge is audited
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Data Integrity; PHI/Privacy; Audit

Title: Prevent duplicate temporary identifiers for concurrent unknown registrations
Preconditions: Two clerks registering unknown patients concurrently
Test_Steps: 1. Both clerks generate unknown-patient identifiers at the same instant | 2. Inspect the assigned identifiers
Expected_Result: Each unknown patient receives a distinct unique identifier with no collision under concurrency

Title: Access to unknown-patient PHI after merge respects break-the-glass audit
Preconditions: Merged patient record; sensitive-access policy active
Test_Steps: 1. Access the merged patient's pre-merge unknown-period data | 2. Provide break-the-glass justification if prompted | 3. Review audit
Expected_Result: Access is permitted with mandatory justification and a complete audit trail recording who accessed merged PHI and why

Title: Activate mass-casualty incident mode (assumption)
Preconditions: ED admin with MCI privilege; MCI mode is an assumed/configurable platform capability
Test_Steps: 1. Activate MCI/disaster mode | 2. Confirm activation banner and altered triage workflow
Expected_Result: System enters MCI mode, displays an active-incident banner, and enables rapid field-triage tagging workflows

Title: START triage tag color decision table (Red/Yellow/Green/Black)
Preconditions: MCI mode active (assumption); START protocol configured
Test_Steps: 1. Apply START criteria for ambulatory status, respiration, perfusion and mental status | 2. Assign tag color per combination
Test_Data: RR>30 -> Red; Ambulatory -> Green; No resp after airway -> Black
Expected_Result: Each START criteria combination maps to the correct tag color (Immediate/Delayed/Minor/Deceased) per the protocol

Title: Bulk register multiple unknown casualties under one incident
Preconditions: MCI mode active (assumption)
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly create several unknown casualty records | 2. Tag each with incident ID and triage color | 3. Verify all linked to the incident
Expected_Result: All casualties are registered with unique temporary IDs, linked to the incident, and individually triage-tagged

Title: Deactivate MCI mode reverts to standard triage workflow
Preconditions: MCI mode active with tagged casualties (assumption)
Test_Steps: 1. Deactivate MCI mode | 2. Confirm | 3. Verify queue/workflow state
Expected_Result: System returns to standard ESI triage workflow; incident records and tags remain available for reporting

Title: Door-to-doctor SLA timer starts at triage completion
Preconditions: Triage completed for an ESI 2 patient; SLA timers configured
Test_Steps: 1. Complete triage | 2. Observe SLA timer start | 3. Verify target window
Expected_Result: A door-to-provider SLA timer starts at triage completion with the acuity-appropriate target window

Title: SLA breach raises a time-critical alert
Preconditions: ESI 2 patient with running SLA timer past target
Test_Steps: 1. Allow the SLA timer to exceed the target without provider assignment | 2. Observe alert
Expected_Result: A time-critical SLA-breach alert is raised to the charge nurse and the patient is highlighted in the queue

Title: Stroke/STEMI clock displays elapsed time-critical interval
Preconditions: Patient on stroke or STEMI pathway
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate the time-critical pathway | 2. Verify elapsed clock from last-known-well/symptom-onset | 3. Confirm threshold markers
Expected_Result: An elapsed time-critical clock is displayed with threshold markers (e.g. thrombolysis window) for the pathway

Title: Alert acknowledgement is captured with actor and timestamp
Preconditions: Active time-critical alert present
Test_Steps: 1. Acknowledge the alert as a clinician | 2. Verify acknowledgement record
Expected_Result: Acknowledgement records the acting user and timestamp; the alert state transitions to Acknowledged and remains in the audit trail

Title: Pairwise alert routing across channel x role x severity
Preconditions: Alert routing rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise combinations of channel {in-app, pager, SMS}, role {nurse, physician}, severity {high, critical} | 2. Verify routing
Expected_Result: Each combination routes to the correct recipients per configuration with no missed or misrouted alert

Title: Triage acuity exported as FHIR Encounter priority/class
Preconditions: Triage encounter saved; FHIR API enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET the Encounter resource for the triage visit | 2. Inspect priority and class elements
Expected_Result: FHIR Encounter exposes the triage priority (e.g. emergency) and class consistent with the assigned acuity

Title: HL7 ADT^A04 emitted on ED registration of unknown patient
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine connected; unknown-patient registration available
Test_Steps: 1. Register an unknown ED patient | 2. Capture the outbound HL7 message
Expected_Result: An HL7 ADT^A04 (register a patient) message is emitted with the temporary identifier and ED point-of-care segment

Title: HL7 ADT^A06/A08 reflects acuity update after re-triage
Preconditions: HL7 interface connected; patient already registered in ED
Test_Steps: 1. Re-triage a patient changing acuity | 2. Capture outbound HL7 message
Expected_Result: An HL7 ADT update (A08/A06) is emitted reflecting the changed acuity/priority without creating a duplicate patient

Title: Reject malformed inbound HL7 triage message
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface active
Test_Steps: 1. Send an HL7 message with a missing required PID segment | 2. Inspect ack
Expected_Result: Interface returns an HL7 application-reject ACK (AR) and the malformed message is not persisted as a patient/triage record

Title: Create triage assessment via REST API with valid payload
Preconditions: API client authenticated with triage write scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST a triage assessment payload | 2. Inspect response and persisted record
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with the created triage resource id; record is retrievable and correctly linked to the patient/encounter

Title: Reject triage API call without authorization
Preconditions: API endpoint reachable; no valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST a triage assessment without a bearer token | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized and no triage record is created

Title: Triage API enforces patient-context tampering protection
Preconditions: Authenticated API client scoped to facility A
Test_Steps: 1. POST a triage assessment referencing a patient outside the caller's authorized facility/context | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API rejects the cross-context reference (403) preventing IDOR and no record is created

Title: Every acuity assignment writes an immutable audit entry
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; triage performed
Test_Steps: 1. Assign and later change an acuity | 2. Query the audit log
Expected_Result: Each assignment and change writes an append-only audit record with actor, timestamp, old and new value; prior entries are not mutated

Title: Triage acuity selection is operable and announced via keyboard and screen reader
Preconditions: Triage form open; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the acuity control with keyboard only | 2. Verify focus order and screen-reader announcement of selected acuity
Expected_Result: Acuity control is fully keyboard-operable with visible focus and the selected acuity level is announced (WCAG 2.1 AA)

Title: Danger-zone vitals flag does not rely on color alone
Preconditions: Triage vitals form with abnormal values
Test_Steps: 1. Enter danger-zone vitals | 2. Inspect the flag presentation
Expected_Result: The danger-zone indication includes a text/icon cue in addition to color, satisfying non-color-reliance accessibility

Title: Exploratory session on rapid re-triage and queue churn under load
Preconditions: Populated ED queue; multiple test patients
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly triage, re-triage and override across several patients | 2. Observe queue ordering, flags and any inconsistencies | 3. Note anomalies
Expected_Result: Queue ordering, acuity flags and SLA timers remain internally consistent; any race, stale-state or display anomaly is documented

Title: Pairwise of vitals-band x age-band x chief-complaint influencing acuity
Preconditions: Triage nurse logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise combinations of vitals-band {normal, danger}, age-band {pediatric, adult, geriatric}, complaint {cardiac, respiratory, trauma} | 2. Record resulting acuity
Expected_Result: Resulting acuity for each combination is consistent with age-specific danger-zone and high-risk rules with no contradictory outcomes
